answers.answer: A) Bob removed it in def456 - check PR #342 for discussion about why the validation was problematic. Reason: git blame shows the last commit that modified a line. Since the validation was removed in def456 by Bob (commit message explicitly states remove email validation), Alice subsequent commit abc123 was refactoring code that already had the validation removed. Bob commit references PR #342 which contains the discussion and rationale for removing the validation.

License Compatibility Analysis: Scenario A (Commercial closed-source): NOT ALLOWED. Problematic: GPL v3 (CSS framework) requires entire project to be open source; SSPL (analytics SDK) requires opening all code used to provide the service. Scenario B (Open source under MIT): NOT ALLOWED. Problematic: GPL v3 is incompatible with MIT licensing - GPL requires derivative works to use GPL. Scenario C (SaaS, no code distribution): PARTIALLY ALLOWED. GPL v3 and LGPL v2.1 do not trigger copyleft in SaaS (no distribution). Problematic: SSPL requires opening all service-related code.
